description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a small crustacean, likely a crayfish or young lobster, with a segmented body featuring a reddish-brown exoskeleton. The cephalothorax is robust and slightly arched, leading to a tapered abdomen with visible segmentation and a fan-shaped tail. Two prominent claws (chelae) extend from the front, with one appearing slightly larger than the other, and several walking legs are visible along the sides. Long, slender antennae project forward from the head, and small, dark eyes are positioned on short stalks. The surface of the exoskeleton appears hard and slightly rough, with subtle color gradations from orange to brown. The overall form is compact and detailed, capturing the natural anatomy and texture of a freshwater crustacean.
description: A small, reddish-brown crustacean with segmented body, prominent claws, long antennae, and a hard, textured exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Left side view showing the full length of the body, both claws, and extended antennae.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the fan-shaped tail, segmented abdomen, and symmetrical leg arrangement.
description_view_2: Right side view displaying the body profile, claws, and antennae from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the claws, head, and forward-projecting antennae.
